message:
fix(config): filter empty path_topology values in merge_config too (audit)

Self-audit pass on ada3019 (rch #10 follow-up) found that
merge_config used a bare `is_some()` check for the path_topology
overlay merge, while apply_layer (in the source-tracking path) and
PathTopologyConfig::to_policy() both treat empty strings as unset.

Surface effect: a project-level .rch/config.toml that left
`canonical_root = ""` (e.g. from an editor template) would clobber a
valid user-level value via `load_config()`'s direct-merge path,
forcing the compiled-in default at runtime even though the source
file the user actually configured was non-empty. The TUI / source-
tracking path (load_config_with_sources_from_paths) wouldn't
exhibit the bug because apply_layer already filters empty.

Tightened the merge_config path_topology branches to use the same
`.as_deref().is_some_and(|s| !s.is_empty())` pattern, so all three
surfaces (merge_config, apply_layer, to_policy) agree on
"empty == unset" semantics.

Verification: cargo build clean; existing path-topology regression
tests still pass; no behavioural change for the common non-empty
configured-value case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
